privileged_info_summary: This task uses synthetic test data for a French medical records system (MedinTux). The task involves updating patient Social Security Numbers (NIR) in a MySQL database by matching entries from a CSV file to patients in the 'DrTuxTest' database using Last Name, First Name, and Date of Birth. Four target patients (MARTIN_Thomas, DURAND_Sophie, PETIT_Julie, MOREL_Claire) have verified NIRs ranging from 160119900100199 to 292093300145689. One distractor patient (LEGRAND_Marc, NIR 160119900100199) exists in the database but not in the CSV file and must not be modified. One distractor name (ROBERT) appears in the CSV but not in the database and should be logged as missing. The CSV file is created at /home/ga/Documents/cpam_corrections.csv with columns: Nom, Prenom, DateNaissance, NouveauNIR. All data is generated deterministically from hardcoded test fixtures in the setup script.
